name: Marketing homepage (/)
description: Public marketing homepage. Adoption-sweep port of marketing.spec.ts 'Homepage (/)' describe block (the structural half: branding heading, key-features section, How-It-Works section, hero download/sign-in CTAs). The 'loads without 500' assertion is already covered by the C8 crawl + the run-level same-origin HTTP-500 invariant; this spec adds the structural element-presence assertions the e2e test made (getByRole heading / feature cards / CTA buttons) so the structural coverage survives the nightly-Playwright cutover. Element ids are the ui-bridge SDK auto-assigned ids captured from a ci-bot CI element-dump (the real render): heading-1 is the hero H1 ('AI development that verifies...'), the key-features section H2 + feature-card H3s, the How-It-Works H2, and the hero CTA buttons. Public page — renders identically authed or unauth, so no requiresUnauthenticated needed (the authed crawl/ spec lane sees it fine). Read-only; structural only.

states.name: Hero section
states.description: Top-of-page hero: the H1 headline. Ports marketing.spec.ts:22/37 (loads + branding heading). NOTE: the header Sign In / Download CTA buttons carry ui-bridge auto-IDs with a render-order index suffix (e.g. button-sign-in-3) that is NOT deterministic across auth states, so we anchor the hero on the stable, unique H1 heading only rather than an index-suffixed button; the unauth Sign-In flow is already covered by the dedicated `login` spec + unauth lane.

assertions.description: Hero H1 headline anchors the page.

states.name: Content sections
states.description: The key narrative section headings rendered down the page: the AI-assisted-development features H2, the How-It-Works H2, and a representative feature-card H3. Ports marketing.spec.ts:73/118 (feature cards + How It Works section).

assertions.description: 'Built for AI-assisted development' features section H2.
